questions.question: What is the primary reason for using two's complement representation for signed integers in most digital computers?
options: To simplify floating-point arithmetic operations.
To allow for easier detection of arithmetic overflow conditions.
To enable both addition and subtraction operations to be performed using a single arithmetic unit (adder) without special handling for signs.
To reduce the number of bits required for storing negative numbers compared to sign-magnitude representation.
To enhance data security by obscuring the true value of negative numbers from casual inspection.

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an Integrated Circuit (IC), what is the main objective of the "Clock Tree Synthesis" (CTS) step?
options: To optimize the power consumption of the entire chip by disabling unused clock signals.
To reduce the number of logic gates by combining multiple clock domains into a single one.
To distribute the clock signal throughout the chip such that it reaches all sequential elements (flip-flops, latches) with minimal skew and delay.
To convert the gate-level netlist into a transistor-level schematic for manufacturing.
To verify the logical functionality of all clock domain crossings (CDCs).

questions.question: Which cache replacement policy removes the block that has not been used for the longest period of time from the cache, typically requiring a mechanism to track the 'age' of each block?
options: Least Frequently Used (LFU)
First-In, First-Out (FIFO)
Random Replacement (RR)
Least Recently Used (LRU)
Most Recently Used (MRU)

questions.question: Which pair of logic gates is considered "universal," meaning that any Boolean function can be implemented using only gates from that specific pair?
options: AND and OR gates
XOR and XNOR gates
NAND and NOR gates
NOT and Buffer gates
Multiplexer (MUX) and Demultiplexer (DEMUX)

questions.question: Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI) is a critical reliability concern in modern CMOS circuits, primarily affecting PMOS transistors under specific stress conditions. What is its main manifestation?
options: A gradual decrease in the threshold voltage (Vt) of PMOS transistors, leading to faster switching times but increased leakage.
A gradual increase in the threshold voltage (Vt) of PMOS transistors, leading to slower switching times and performance degradation.
An accelerated breakdown of the gate oxide in NMOS transistors due to high electric fields.
The transport of metal atoms in interconnect lines, causing shorts or opens at elevated temperatures.
Intermittent failures caused by random cosmic ray particle strikes, leading to soft errors in memory elements.
